fields.description:
## Why

E6 measures whether the plan-review gate's **LLM-as-judge is reliable** — the same
judgment, re-run, must land the same way. Two documented judge failure modes threaten
the shared Pass-2 verify / Pass-3 decide vocabulary that R3 and R5 build on:

- **Self-consistency drift.** An unstable per-finding decision injects noise into the
  Pass-2 sub-answer vocabulary. R5 adds a new Pass-2 sub-answer
  (`asserted_capability_confirmed`) routed over the G6/E4/T3 cohort
  (`docs/research/task-decomposition-sota-2026.md:195-199`); it is only worth adding if
  the judge that emits it is stable across repeats. Our own research substrate records
  that "~3-vote self-consistency captures most of the available gain (+4pp, saturating)"
  and that "judges are systematically overconfident" (`sota:44`; refs at `sota:241` —
  Wang et al., *LLMs are not Fair Evaluators*, ACL 2024, arXiv:2305.17926; Zheng et al.,
  MT-Bench, arXiv:2306.05685; judge overconfidence, arXiv:2508.06225).
- **Order sensitivity.** Section-order permutation of the input plan should not flip the
  gate verdict. `sota:44` records order-swap flipping 82.5% of *pairwise* verdicts (Wang,
  ACL 2024). Our gate already uses the recommended mitigation — absolute rubric scoring,
  never pairwise — so E6 empirically confirms the **residual** order-sensitivity of the
  actual gate is below a floor before R3 lands a new (order-exposed) container criterion.

E6 **re-judges an existing sample and measures agreement (reliability), not accuracy
against gold** (`runs/README.md:154-156`: "E6 re-judges these findings for
self-consistency; it never needed the TP/FP labels"). This is why E6 survived the E1
descope (ticket comment 2026-07-16): re-running the gate's own judge to measure its
*stochastic stability* is the textbook definition of a self-consistency test — it is
**not** the circular-adjudication error that invalidated E1's finding-level TP/FP
labeling (which mis-used the gate's judge as *gold truth for accuracy*). E6 never treats
any judge output as truth; it only compares repeated judge outputs to each other.

E6 gates two downstream stories: R3 (with E2) and R5 (with E5).

## What

Two bounded judge-reliability experiments, each with an explicit metric + pass/fail
floor, run over **committed, reproducible samples**. No `src/rebar` behavior changes —
E6 is measurement over the *existing* gate (Pass-2 verify → Pass-3 decide →
`orchestrator.finalize_verdict`).

### Grounded definitions (verified against the code/corpus, not assumed)

- **"decision"** (self-consistency unit of agreement) = the Pass-3 output field
  `decision ∈ {block, advisory, dropped, indeterminate}` produced by
  `three_pass.pass3_decide(...)` (the deterministic decision path; harness
  `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/harnesses/three_pass.py`), whose vocabulary matches
  the persisted sidecar `decision` enum. Pass-3 is deterministic given Pass-2, so all
  measured variance is the judge's Pass-2 stochasticity.
- **"verdict"** (order-shuffle unit of agreement) = the gate's overall
  `verdict ∈ {PASS, BLOCK, INDETERMINATE}` returned by
  `rebar.llm.plan_review.orchestrator.finalize_verdict` (`orchestrator.py:467-495`;
  surfaced as `{verdict: PASS|BLOCK|INDETERMINATE, …}` at
  `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/__init__.py:408`). A `PASS↔BLOCK` or `PASS↔INDETERMINATE`
  flip across permutations is a disagreement. **Infra-INDETERMINATE** (LLM tier
  unavailable, `llm_unavailable`) is *not* judge instability — such runs are excluded and
  re-run so only judge-produced verdicts enter the metric.

### Experiment A — self-consistency of the Pass-2/Pass-3 judge over the R5 cohort

- **Sample frame (concrete).** `runs/adjudication_corpus.jsonl` rows filtered by
  `criterion ∈ {G6, E4, T3}` — **exactly the R5 sub-answer's own routing cohort**
  (`sota:195-199` routes G6/E4/T3 through the agentic verifier for
  `asserted_capability_confirmed`). Verified in the committed file: **74 rows across 66
  distinct tickets** (advisory 47 / dropped 20 / block 7) — the cohort exists and clears
  the N≥50 floor with 24 rows of headroom. (Chosen over the CLAUDE.md "G1G2+E4" framing
  because E6 gates *R5*, and R5 itself is defined at `sota:195-199` as the G6/E4/T3
  cohort; the corpus has no `asserted_capability` label, so a criterion-set filter is the
  only grounded slice.)
- **N = 50** findings, sampled deterministically (fixed seed) from the 74-member cohort,
  deduped by `(ticket_id, finding_id)` (finding_id alone is non-unique: 388 unique of
  400). The 24-row headroom absorbs any ticket whose plan text cannot be snapshotted.
- **Re-judge inputs are frozen and committed** to defeat the "no plan-text corpus" gap:
  a build step snapshots, once, each sampled finding's ticket plan text via
  `rebar show <ticket_id>` (the replay-derived `description` == the reviewed plan) plus
  the finding record, writing `runs/e6_selfconsistency_inputs.jsonl`
  (`{finding_id, ticket_id, title, plan, criterion, finding, location, suggested_fix}`).
  A ticket whose plan is unretrievable (compacted/archived) is skipped and the sample is
  topped up deterministically from the cohort remainder to hold N=50. The N-vote harness
  reads **only this committed file** — no live-store dependency at judge time.
- **Judge run.** For each frozen input, re-run the **agentic** Pass-2 verifier
  (`three_pass.pass2_verify(title, plan, finding, repo_root, agentic=True)` → the R5
  cohort's `plan_review_verifier_agentic` path) then deterministic
  `pass3_decide(...)`, **3 votes** (`sota:44`: 3-vote self-consistency saturates the
  gain). Record every vote's `decision` and the **existing** Pass-2 binary sub-answers in
  the current `PASS2_TOOL`/`GRADED` schema (`three_pass.py`). `asserted_capability_confirmed`
  is **R5's future addition** and does NOT exist yet — E6 does not record it; E6 measures the
  reliability of the *existing* judge, which is the prerequisite R5 depends on. The agreement
  metric (below) is computed over `decision`, the sub-answer that already exists for this
  cohort — not over any R5 field.
- **Metric + floor.** Over the N findings × 3 votes, compute **Fleiss' κ** (raters = the
  3 interchangeable votes; subjects = findings; categories = the 4-value `decision`
  space) **and raw exact-agreement** (fraction of findings where all 3 votes are
  identical). **PASS iff κ ≥ 0.6 (Landis–Koch "substantial") AND raw agreement ≥ 0.8;**
  below **either** → FAIL, recorded as an **R5 blocker**. Both floors are reported
  because κ deflates under skewed base rates (the cohort is advisory-heavy), so raw
  agreement is the prevalence-robust co-floor.

### Experiment B — order-shuffle stability of the gate verdict

- **Sample frame (concrete).** `runs/corpus_sample.json` — 19 committed plan-review
  inputs that **carry full plan text** (the only committed plan-text corpus; the outcome
  and adjudication corpora store no plan text). Verified: of the 19, **15 have ≥2
  top-level `##` sections** (the permutable set); the 4 single-section plans are excluded
  from the shuffle denominator (no meaningful permutation). **N = 15 plans.**
- **The "≥20 container plans" contradiction is resolved by DROPPING the
  container-specific requirement.** Only 5 of the 19 inputs are containers
  (`has_children=true`: 3 epics + 2 stories) and the run corpora hold exactly one
  container-type ticket — a container-only shuffle is under-powered and irreproducible.
  Order-sensitivity is a **general** judge property: if the gate verdict is unstable under
  section shuffle at large, any new criterion (including R3's container criterion)
  inherits that instability, so a stable general-plan result is a **prerequisite** for
  trusting R3. The 5-plan container subset is reported **descriptively only** (not a
  gate).
- **Mechanism.** For each plan, generate **3 section-order permutations** by reordering
  its top-level `##` section blocks (content preserved verbatim; a pure helper
  `permute_sections`), then run the full gate verdict path
  (Pass-1 → Pass-2 → Pass-3 → `finalize_verdict`) on each permutation and record the
  `verdict`. Frozen permutation specs are committed to `runs/e6_ordershuffle_inputs.jsonl`.
- **Metric + floor.** Over 15 plans × 3 permutations, compute **Fleiss' κ** and **raw
  exact-agreement** on the `verdict` categories. **PASS iff κ ≥ 0.6 AND raw agreement ≥
  0.8;** below → FAIL, recorded as an **R3 prerequisite failure**. Secondary descriptive
  metric: mean per-plan Jaccard of the fired-criteria set across permutations (not gated).

### LLM-spend honesty

E6 **does** make repeated live, billable LLM calls — self-consistency and order-shuffle
inherently require re-runs; there is no persisted-sidecar shortcut for a *stability*
measurement. Spend is bounded and stated: **A = 50 findings × 3 votes = 150 agentic
Pass-2 calls** (Pass-3 is deterministic/free); **B = 15 plans × 3 permutations = 45
full-gate runs**. This is deliberately a bounded sub-sample — not the 400-finding
adjudication corpus nor the 628-row outcome corpus.

## Scope

- **`docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/harnesses/e6_judge_reliability.py`** — the driver:
  builds frozen inputs (Exp A snapshot + Exp B permutations), runs the votes/permutations,
  and writes the agreement tables + summary. Reuses `three_pass.pass2_verify` /
  `pass3_decide` and `orchestrator.finalize_verdict`; does not modify them.
- **`docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/`** — committed inputs + outputs (all paths
  in the ACs below).
- **`tests/unit/test_e6_agreement.py`** — CI-collectable golden test for the pure helpers
  (`fleiss_kappa`, `raw_agreement`, `permute_sections`) on fixed fixtures; **no LLM**.
- **README writeup** — a new "E6 — judge reliability" section in
  `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/README.md`.
- **No `src/rebar` changes.** E6 measures the existing gate; R3/R5 later consume E6's
  recorded verdicts. Consumes `runs/adjudication_corpus.jsonl` (Exp A cohort) and
  `runs/corpus_sample.json` (Exp B plans), both already committed.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **Exp A run recorded.** `runs/e6_selfconsistency_inputs.jsonl` (N≥50 frozen
  cohort inputs, each `criterion ∈ {G6,E4,T3}` with non-empty `plan`) and
  `runs/e6_selfconsistency.jsonl` (3 votes/finding, each with `decision` and Pass-2
  binary sub-answers) both exist and are consistent.
  Proving:
  `python3 -c "import json; ins=[json.loads(l) for l in open('docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/e6_selfconsistency_inputs.jsonl')]; assert len(ins)>=50 and all(r['criterion'] in {'G6','E4','T3'} and r['plan'] for r in ins); v=[json.loads(l) for l in open('docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/e6_selfconsistency.jsonl')]; ids={r['finding_id'] for r in ins}; per={i:0 for i in ids}; [per.__setitem__(r['finding_id'], per[r['finding_id']]+1) for r in v]; assert all(c==3 for c in per.values()), per; print('OK', len(ins), 'findings x3 votes')"`

- [ ] **Exp A passes its floor (self-consistency).**
  `runs/e6_selfconsistency_agreement.csv` (one row per finding:
  `finding_id,criterion,decision_1,decision_2,decision_3,all_agree,modal_decision`) and
  `runs/e6_summary.json` record **Fleiss' κ AND raw agreement**; the summary states
  `self_consistency.pass == (kappa>=0.6 and raw_agreement>=0.8)`. A sub-floor result is
  recorded verbatim as an **R5 blocker** (not silently passed).
  Proving:
  `python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/e6_summary.json'))['self_consistency']; assert d['n_findings']>=50 and set(('fleiss_kappa','raw_agreement','pass'))<=set(d) and d['pass']==(d['fleiss_kappa']>=0.6 and d['raw_agreement']>=0.8); print('self-consistency', d)"`

- [ ] **Exp B run recorded (order-shuffle over the committed plan-text corpus).**
  `runs/e6_ordershuffle_inputs.jsonl` (the ≥15 plans from `corpus_sample.json` with ≥2
  top-level sections, each with 3 section-order permutations) and
  `runs/e6_ordershuffle.jsonl` (one `verdict ∈ {PASS,BLOCK,INDETERMINATE}` per
  plan×permutation, judge-produced infra-INDETERMINATE excluded) exist and are complete.
  Proving:
  `python3 -c "import json,collections; ins=[json.loads(l) for l in open('docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/e6_ordershuffle_inputs.jsonl')]; c=collections.Counter(r['plan_id'] for r in ins); assert len(c)>=15 and all(n>=3 for n in c.values()); o=[json.loads(l) for l in open('docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/e6_ordershuffle.jsonl')]; assert all(r['verdict'] in {'PASS','BLOCK','INDETERMINATE'} for r in o); print('OK', len(c), 'plans x>=3 permutations')"`

- [ ] **Exp B passes its floor (order-shuffle stability), with an explicit pass/fail
  threshold symmetrical with Exp A.** `runs/e6_ordershuffle_agreement.csv` (one row per
  plan: `plan_id,verdict_1,verdict_2,verdict_3,all_agree,modal_verdict`) and
  `runs/e6_summary.json` record **Fleiss' κ AND raw agreement** on `verdict`;
  `order_shuffle.pass == (kappa>=0.6 and raw_agreement>=0.8)`. A sub-floor result is
  recorded verbatim as an **R3 prerequisite failure**.
  Proving:
  `python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/e6_summary.json'))['order_shuffle']; assert d['n_plans']>=15 and set(('fleiss_kappa','raw_agreement','pass'))<=set(d) and d['pass']==(d['fleiss_kappa']>=0.6 and d['raw_agreement']>=0.8); print('order-shuffle', d)"`

- [ ] **Pure agreement/permutation helpers are unit-tested (CI-collectable, no LLM).**
  `tests/unit/test_e6_agreement.py` asserts `fleiss_kappa` and `raw_agreement` on
  hand-computed fixtures (incl. a perfect-agreement fixture → κ==1.0, raw==1.0, and a
  known-κ table) and that `permute_sections` preserves content while reordering `##`
  blocks.
  Proving: `python -m pytest tests/unit/test_e6_agreement.py -q`

- [ ] **Writeup records provenance for R3 and R5.** The new "E6" section in
  `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/README.md` records: the exact cohort filter
  (`criterion ∈ {G6,E4,T3}`, N and counts), the Exp B sample (`corpus_sample.json`, the
  15-plan permutable subset, and why the container-specific ≥20 requirement was dropped),
  the metric definitions + floors (κ≥0.6 AND raw≥0.8), the prompts-as-run reference, the
  bounded LLM-spend figures, and the two gate-input verdicts
  (`self_consistency.pass` → R5, `order_shuffle.pass` → R3).
  Proving:
  `grep -Eq 'criterion.*(G6|E4|T3)' docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/README.md && grep -Eq 'kappa|Fleiss' docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/README.md && grep -Eq 'corpus_sample' docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/README.md && echo OK`
